topic_title: Dictionaries — key-value pairs for structured data
verdict: PASS — cycle 1. Side-by-side panel layout renders correctly. Left panel (blue) shows list with three indexed slots [0]=72 / [1]=88 / [2]=65; right panel (green) shows dictionary with three labelled key-value slots 'name'→Alice / 'score'→88 / 'grade'→B; gold 'vs' hub in centre. The index-vs-key contrast is immediately visible to a zero-background learner. No scope creep (no nested dicts, API patterns, or LLM nodes). All 7 nodes carry plain-language labels. Colour coding is consistent with project palette (blue=list, green=dict, gold=contrast marker). PNG rendered at 12 KB via mmdc — visually inspected and passes.

label_clarity.notes: All labels are plain English. No unexplained acronyms. Index notation [0]/[1]/[2] matches corpus §5.2 syntax. Key-value notation matches corpus §5.1 vocabulary.

scope_discipline.notes: All nodes are within 12.5 concept space (list-index vs dict-key). No nested dict nodes, no API/LLM/RAG topology, no advanced operations beyond the headline.

layout_intent.intent_match: Side-by-side visual of a list (numbered slots) vs a dictionary (labelled key→value slots) — fully realised in the render.

beginner_readability.notes: Contrast is visually immediate. A learner who has never seen a dictionary can tell at a glance: left = numbers, right = names. Colour coding reinforces the distinction.

png_render.notes: PNG rendered cleanly. No overlapping nodes, no truncated labels, no empty subgraph shells.
